perturbation.original_text: A total of 100,000 of the Company's restricted common stock issued under and pursuant to the terms of Regulation D under the Securities Act of 1933 (the "Shares") shall be issued upon execution of this contract provided that the issuance of the Shares shall be subject to prior approval of the Board of Directors. The company shall cause its counsel to provide an opinion letter for removal of any legend when and if such legend may be removed in accordance with applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: A total of 100,000 of the Company's restricted common stock shall be issued upon execution of this contract. The company shall cause its counsel to provide an opinion letter.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'issued under and pursuant to the terms of Regulation D under the Securities Act of 1933 (the "Shares") provided that the issuance of the Shares shall be subject to prior approval of the Board of Directors when and if such legend may be removed in accordance with applicable law.', the text creates uncertainty about the regulatory compliance and the specific conditions under which the shares are issued. This change contradicts with Section 10, which states 'Consultant hereby represents that it has in place policies and procedures relating to, and addressing, with the commercially reasonable intent to ensure compliance with, all applicable securities laws, rules and regulations'. If it's not clear if Shares fall under Regulation D, the SEC & LEGAL COMPLIANCE section becomes dubious.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SEC & LEGAL COMPLIANCE. Consultant hereby represents that it has in place policies and procedures relating to, and addressing, with the commercially reasonable intent to ensure compliance with, all applicable securities laws, rules and regulations, including but not limited to the use, release or other publication of forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act and Section 21E of the Exchange Act and disclosure requirements outlined in Section 17B of the Exchange Act regarding the required disclosure of the nature and terms of its relationship with the Company in any and all literature or other communication(s) relating to these services, including but not limited to: Press Releases, letters to investors and telephone or other personal communication(s) with potential or current investors. In addition, Consultant acknowledges that United States securities laws and the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder prohibit any person with material, non- public information about a company from purchasing, selling, trading, or entering into options, puts, calls or other derivatives in respect of securities of such issuer or from communicating such information to any other person or entity and Consultant shall comply with such laws, rules and regulations. Consultant will take no action to manipulate the market price of the Company's securities in violation of law or regulation, nor pay or otherwise induce others to take any such actions, nor publish comments about the Company without appropriately disclosing payments received therefor.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the issuance of shares was subject to Regulation D and Board of Directors approval. The changed text removes these conditions. This creates a direct contradiction with the 'SEC & LEGAL COMPLIANCE' section, which states 'Consultant hereby represents that it has in place policies and procedures relating to, and addressing, with the commercially reasonable intent to ensure compliance with, all applicable securities laws, rules and regulations'. By omitting the reference to Regulation D, the text introduces ambiguity about the regulatory compliance of the share issuance, potentially conflicting with the consultant's representation of compliance with all applicable securities laws. Since it is no longer clear if shares are subject to Board of Director's approval, it raises a compliance concern.

perturbation.original_text: A one time issuance of 50,000 Common Stock options priced at the Nasdaq Official Closing Price ("NCOP") on the date of approval of the Board of Directors of the company. All options shall vest monthly for six months.
perturbation.changed_text: A one time issuance of 50,000 Common Stock options. All options shall vest monthly.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of "priced at the Nasdaq Official Closing Price ("NCOP") on the date of approval of the Board of Directors of the company. for six months." eliminates the reference to the pricing mechanism of the stock options. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because it introduces ambiguity regarding how the stock options' value will be determined, creating potential conflicts and enforcement issues. This change impacts the SEC & LEGAL COMPLIANCE Section, since its not defined how is being calculated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SEC & LEGAL COMPLIANCE. Consultant hereby represents that it has in place policies and procedures relating to, and addressing, with the commercially reasonable intent to ensure compliance with, all applicable securities laws, rules and regulations, including but not limited to the use, release or other publication of forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act and Section 21E of the Exchange Act and disclosure requirements outlined in Section 17B of the Exchange Act regarding the required disclosure of the nature and terms of its relationship with the Company in any and all literature or other communication(s) relating to these services, including but not limited to: Press Releases, letters to investors and telephone or other personal communication(s) with potential or current investors. In addition, Consultant acknowledges that United States securities laws and the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder prohibit any person with material, non- public information about a company from purchasing, selling, trading, or entering into options, puts, calls or other derivatives in respect of securities of such issuer or from communicating such information to any other person or entity and Consultant shall comply with such laws, rules and regulations. Consultant will take no action to manipulate the market price of the Company's securities in violation of law or regulation, nor pay or otherwise induce others to take any such actions, nor publish comments about the Company without appropriately disclosing payments received therefor.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the stock options would be priced at the Nasdaq Official Closing Price (NCOP) on the date of Board approval. The changed text removes this pricing mechanism, creating ambiguity about how the options' value is determined. This omission introduces a contradiction with the SEC & LEGAL COMPLIANCE Section, because without a clear pricing mechanism, it is difficult to ensure compliance with securities laws related to insider trading and market manipulation, as detailed in that section. The SEC section expects clarity and compliance with securities laws, which is directly impacted by not knowing the pricing mechanism. Therefore, it's a strong in-text contradiction.
